name: incus-tui
//: incus-tui develops against a real local Incus daemon that boots VMs. That needs a
//2: privileged, KVM-capable container: hardware virtualization (/dev/kvm), vsock
//3: (/dev/vhost-vsock), tun, and the full capability set. runArgs documents those host
//4: requirements; scripts/setup-dev.sh installs Incus and start-incusd.sh (re)starts the
//5: daemon on each container start (there is no systemd to do it).
